1 
PURPOSE AND SCOPE  
1.1 SCOPE OF DOCUMENT 
An onboard UHF radio communication system is installed to provide reliable short-range voice communication 
throughout the facility, supporting routine operations, maintenance coordination, and emergency response activities. 
The system ensures continuous coverage across indoor compartments, accommodation areas, machinery spaces, and 
exposed deck locations. 
The core of the system is housed in a dedicated communications cabinet, which accommodates the UHF repeaters, RF 
combiner, server, control stations, and associated networking and power distribution equipment. This centralized 
arrangement enables efficient signal management, system monitoring, and simplified maintenance. 
Radio coverage is achieved through a combination of indoor and outdoor antenna infrastructure. Indoor areas are 
served by multiple ceiling-mounted UHF antennas connected via RF feeder cables and ½-inch leaky feeder cables, 
providing distributed signal radiation and consistent coverage inside enclosed spaces and passageways. Outdoor deck 
and open areas are covered by marine-grade external antennas installed at elevated positions to maximize 
transmission range and minimize shadowing from vessel structures. 
All antennas are interconnected using low-loss RF400 maritime coaxial cables to reduce signal attenuation and 
maintain communication quality across the network. 
Fixed communication points are provided through UHF base stations installed at strategic operational locations, 
enabling continuous hands-free or desk-mounted operation. Portable intrinsically safe ATEX handheld radios are 
issued to personnel, allowing safe and effective communication in hazardous and classified areas.  
This Installation, Operation, and Maintenance (IOM) Manual provides guidelines for the safe installation, configuration, 
operation, and maintenance of the UHF Communication System installed onboard. The document is intended for use 
by commissioning engineers, operations personnel, and maintenance technicians.

Each of the main components of the system is described in more detail below 
1.3 EQUIPMENT DESCRIPTION 
1.3.1 
UHF REPEATER MOTOROLA MOTOTRBO SLR5500 
The Motorola MOTOTRBO SLR5500 is a high-performance, professional two-way radio repeater designed to extend 
the coverage, capacity, and reliability of onboard UHF communication systems. It operates in both UHF and VHF bands, 
supporting frequencies including 400–470 MHz for the UHF model, and accommodates up to 64 programmable 
channels with RF output power up to 50 W.  
The SLR5500 supports both analog FM and digital MOTOTRBO DMR modes, providing compatibility with legacy analog 
radios as well as advanced digital communications. In digital mode, it uses efficient two-slot TDMA technology to 
deliver two simultaneous voice paths on a single 12.5 kHz channel, effectively doubling capacity and improving 
spectrum utilization.  
Constructed in a compact 1U rack-mount form factor, the repeater incorporates an integrated power supply and 3 A 
battery charger, enabling simplified installation within communication racks or cabinets. It is designed for continuous 
100% duty cycle operation, with robust thermal management and energy-efficient performance that reduces 
operating costs.  
The unit includes advanced features such as digital error correction, repeater diagnostics and control, clear LED status 
indicators, and IP interface connectivity for remote management and network integration. 
MOTOTRBO SLR5500 installed at the following location:       
▪ 
A DECK-TELECOM ROOM-UHF Cabinet (86-RX-017) 
1.3.2 
TRANSMISSION AND RECEIVER SYSTEM 
The UIY Transmission and Receiver System (model UIY4T4R3UB2U) is a passive RF combining and distribution unit 
designed to integrate multiple UHF transmitters and receivers into a common antenna system while maintaining safe 
isolation and stable signal performance. The assembly incorporates internal hybrid combiners, duplexing/filtering 
networks, and impedance-matched RF paths that allow several radios or repeater channels to operate simultaneously 
without mutual interference. 
The unit provides low insertion loss on both transmit and receive paths to preserve output power and receiver 
sensitivity, while high port-to-port isolation minimizes intermodulation, desensitization, and crosstalk between 
channels. This ensures reliable communication quality, particularly in multi-channel repeater or base station 
installations where multiple frequencies are active at the same time. 
By enabling antenna sharing, the system reduces the number of separate antennas, feeders, and deck penetrations 
required, simplifying installation and lowering structural loading on masts. The compact, shielded metal enclosure with 
standard RF connectors is suitable for continuous duty operation in harsh marine and industrial environments, offering 
improved reliability, easier maintenance, and efficient spectrum utilization.

1f5da022-8c48-48a5-84d9-fe295a569b5c.text:
EMEM FPSO 
WCPOKWTP-450-TE-(VAE) MN-021 
 
 
[Comments] 
Telecom System IOM – UHF SYSTEM

Status: IFR 
 
      
This document is the property of Contractor and should not be distributed, copied, quoted or reproduced in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, without the prior 
written consent of Contractor. This document is issued as an “uncontrolled copy” unless over-stamped “CONTROLLED COPY”. 
Overall, the unit functions as a central RF combining and protection interface between radios/repeaters and the 
antenna system, supporting safe, efficient, and space-saving multi-channel UHF communication onboard. 
RF combiner installed at the following location:       
▪ 
A DECK-TELECOM ROOM-UHF Cabinet (86-RX-017) 
1.3.3 
CONTROL STATION MOTOROLA DM4400E 
The Motorola DM4400e is a digital mobile radio configured as a fixed control station and permanently installed inside 
the UF cabinet as part of the ship’s onboard UHF communication system. The unit is integrated with the vessel’s TRBO 
Net Server, which acts as the central controller for the MOTOTRBO digital network, enabling routing, monitoring, and 
management of all UHF voice communications across the ship. Through this connection, the DM4400e provides 
reliable two-way communication between the bridge or control room and all portable and mobile radios carried by 
ship personnel. Each radio on the network is assigned a unique digital ID, allowing the TRBO server to automatically 
route individual or group calls to the intended recipient, ensuring clear, organized, and interference-free 
communication throughout the vessel. Incoming calls to the control station are received via the network and can 
trigger external alerts, while operators can respond directly using the microphone and front-panel controls to initiate 
calls, select channels, and manage talk groups. 
In addition to the primary control station, two dedicated DM4400e units are separately interfaced with the ship’s PAGA 
(Public Address and General Alarm) System A and System B, providing communication interconnection between the 
UHF radio network and the PAGA broadcast systems. These radios function as fixed interface stations, enabling voice 
audio from the UHF network to be transmitted through the respective PAGA channels for wide-area announcements 
or emergency communication. By maintaining separate interfaces for PAGA A and PAGA B, redundancy is achieved, 
ensuring continuous operation and system availability in the event of a single channel failure. This arrangement allows 
personnel using handheld or mobile UHF radios to communicate directly with the PAGA system via the control stations, 
thereby supporting both routine coordination and emergency broadcasting across all vessel spaces. The fixed 
installation of all DM4400e units within the UF cabinet provides a protected environment, stable power supply, and 
continuous network connectivity, ensuring dependable performance and centralized control of shipboard UHF and 
PAGA communications. 
Three Motorola DM4400e installed at the following location:    
▪ 
A DECK-TELECOM ROOM-UHF Cabinet (86-RX-017) 
1.3.4 
NETWORK SWITCH -D-LINK DGS-1024C 
The D-Link DGS-1024C is a 24-port Gigabit Ethernet unmanaged network switch designed to provide high-speed wired 
connectivity for local area networks. It features twenty-four 10/100/1000 Mbps copper LAN ports that support 
automatic negotiation and auto MDI/MDIX, eliminating the need for crossover cables and simplifying network cabling. 
The switch operates at full wire-speed with a store-and-forward switching method and a switching capacity of 48 Gbps, 
enabling reliable data transmission with low latency across all connected devices.  
As an unmanaged plug-and-play device, the DGS-1024C requires no user configuration, making setup straightforward 
and maintenance free. Each port includes LED indicators to show link status, activity, and speed, allowing easy visual 
monitoring of network connections. The switch also supports IEEE 802.1p QoS to prioritise time-sensitive traffic and 
IEEE 802.3x flow control to minimise packet loss under load.

EMEM FPSO 
WCPOKWTP-450-TE-(VAE) MN-021 
 
 
[Comments]

Page 8 of 56

This document is the property of Contractor and should not be distributed, copied, quoted or reproduced in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, without the prior 
written consent of Contractor. This document is issued as an “uncontrolled copy” unless over-stamped “CONTROLLED COPY”. 
The unit incorporates IEEE 802.3az Energy-Efficient Ethernet (EEE) for reduced power usage, adjusting power 
dynamically based on network traffic and cable length to improve energy efficiency and lower operating costs. Its fan 
less design ensures silent operation and its rugged metal housing supports stable performance in typical network 
environments.  
Overall, the D-Link DGS-1024C provides a cost-effective, high-performance switching solution for expanding network 
capacity, interconnecting servers, workstations, and communication devices with high data throughput and robust 
reliability 
D-Link DGS-1024C installed at the following location:    
▪ 
A DECK-TELECOM ROOM-UHF Cabinet (86-RX-017) 
1.3.5 
TRBO NET SERVER HP PRODESK 400 G9 SFF I5 
The HP ProDesk 400 G9 Small Form Factor (SFF), powered by an Intel Core i5 processor, is deployed as the TRBO Net 
Server to serve as the central communication and data processing platform for the UHF MOTOTRBO network and 
associated system infrastructure. This server functions as the primary host for the TRBO network management 
software, system logging, network routing, database services, and integration interfaces required for managing 
distributed radio communications onboard. 
The HP ProDesk 400 G9 SFF delivers a balanced combination of processing performance, memory capacity, and I/O 
connectivity in a compact chassis suitable for installation within the communications or server rack. Its Intel Core i5 
CPU provides multi-tasking capability essential for handling real-time radio traffic management, database operations, 
and logging functions without communication delays. The unit’s integrated storage subsystem supports local data 
retention for system logs, configuration backups, and operational history, while standard Ethernet interfaces provide 
high-speed connectivity to network switches. 
As the core of the TRBO Net Server architecture, the ProDesk 400 G9 hosts and executes the TRBO Net software stack, 
which manages digital radio registration, network bridging, talk group routing, and subscriber authentication. It also 
interfaces with the UHF repeater infrastructure, firewall, and system monitoring tools to ensure coordinated network 
performance, fault detection, and security compliance. The server’s Small Form Factor design minimizes rack footprint 
while maintaining serviceability through accessible internal components and external peripheral ports.

1.3.5 
TRBO NET SERVER HP PRODESK 400 G9 SFF I5 
The HP ProDesk 400 G9 Small Form Factor (SFF), powered by an Intel Core i5 processor, is deployed as the TRBO Net 
Server to serve as the central communication and data processing platform for the UHF MOTOTRBO network and 
associated system infrastructure. This server functions as the primary host for the TRBO network management 
software, system logging, network routing, database services, and integration interfaces required for managing 
distributed radio communications onboard. 
The HP ProDesk 400 G9 SFF delivers a balanced combination of processing performance, memory capacity, and I/O 
connectivity in a compact chassis suitable for installation within the communications or server rack. Its Intel Core i5 
CPU provides multi-tasking capability essential for handling real-time radio traffic management, database operations, 
and logging functions without communication delays. The unit’s integrated storage subsystem supports local data 
retention for system logs, configuration backups, and operational history, while standard Ethernet interfaces provide 
high-speed connectivity to network switches. 
As the core of the TRBO Net Server architecture, the ProDesk 400 G9 hosts and executes the TRBO Net software stack, 
which manages digital radio registration, network bridging, talk group routing, and subscriber authentication. It also 
interfaces with the UHF repeater infrastructure, firewall, and system monitoring tools to ensure coordinated network 
performance, fault detection, and security compliance. The server’s Small Form Factor design minimizes rack footprint 
while maintaining serviceability through accessible internal components and external peripheral ports.

1.3.10 
INDOOR UHF ANTENNA 
The Kenbotong TDJ-450BKB-Y is an indoor UHF wideband antenna engineered to support reliable voice communication 
within enclosed environments such as accommodation areas, corridors, control rooms, and crew spaces. Operating 
across the UHF frequency range 450 -470 MHz, this antenna is optimized for use with onboard UHF communication 
systems, providing uniform signal coverage, minimal reflection, and effective signal propagation within indoor spaces 
where direct outdoor antenna coverage may be compromised. The design of the TDJ-450BKB-Y emphasizes 
compactness and ease of installation. Its low-profile form factor allows it to be mounted unobtrusively on bulkheads, 
ceilings, or equipment compartments without interfering with normal operations or interior layouts. The antenna’s 
internal radiating elements are tuned to deliver consistent performance across the specified band, ensuring 
dependable reception and transmission with minimal standing-wave ratio (SWR) when installed in accordance with 
manufacturer mounting recommendations. 
Constructed with robust materials and quality connectors, the unit interfaces with the onboard UHF repeater and 
distribution system through standard coaxial feeder cable, ensuring low-loss signal transfer and compatibility with 
typical 50 Ω RF systems. Due to its indoor placement, the TDJ-450BKB-Y contributes to enhanced communication 
coverage, reducing dead zones and improving overall system reliability within internal compartments. 
This antenna is particularly suitable for use as part of a distributed antenna system, where multiple indoor antennas 
are deployed to achieve consistent UHF coverage throughout the living, working, and operational spaces on board 
vessels or offshore facilities.

1.3.11 
OUTDOOR UHF ANTENNA AMPHENOL PROCOM CXL70-3LW/L 
The Amphenol Procom CXL 70-3LW is a vertically polarized, omnidirectional UHF base station and marine antenna 
providing 3 dB gain and reliable wide-area coverage across the UHF band. The radiating element is carefully engineered 
and fully sealed inside a high-quality conical fiberglass tube, ensuring stable electrical performance with low wind load 
and excellent resistance to corrosion in marine and outdoor environments. The antenna offers wide bandwidth with 
respect to both VSWR and gain, enabling efficient power transfer and consistent communication quality. The unit is 
supplied with a sturdy lightweight mast mount, manufactured from epoxy-coated, non-corrosive aluminium. All 
metallic parts are DC-grounded to reduce electrical noise and static build-up from atmospheric discharges, resulting in 
improved receiver performance and protection. The overall design is vibration-proof, lightweight, slim-line, and 
corrosion-resistant, making the CXL 70-3LW well suited for dependable long-term operation in marine and industrial 
communication systems. 
Procom CXL70-3LW/l installed at the following location: 
▪ 
COMPASS DECK (86-RU-426) 
▪ 
NAV.DECK (86-RX-421/86-RX-422/86-RX-423/86-RX-424) 
1.3.12 
UHF BASE STATION RADIO MOTOTRBO DM4600E 
The Motorola MOTOTRBO DM4600e is a robust, high-performance UHF two-way mobile/base station radio engineered 
for professional voice and data communications in both analog FM and digital DMR (MOTOTRBO) modes. It operates 
within the UHF frequency range (typically 403–470 MHz) and supports up to 1000 programmable channels, making it 
suitable for extensive fleet, fixed-station, or base communication requirements where reliable, scalable radio coverage 
is essential. Featuring a 4-line alphanumeric colour display and intuitive menu-driven user interface, the DM4600e 
provides clear status information and operational feedback. A set of four programmable buttons allows operators to 
quickly access commonly used functions, enhancing usability in dynamic environments. Built-in multi-coloured LED 
indicators and a high-power internal speaker deliver clear visual and audio feedback for channel activity and 
communications status. The radio supports advanced digital features including group, individual, and all-call voice calls, 
optional encryption, and adaptive audio functions such as Intelligent Audio, which automatically adjusts volume based 
on ambient noise conditions. Combined analog and digital capability ensure backward compatibility and seamless 
transition from legacy systems to advanced digital communications.  
Designed for demanding operational environments, the DM4600e is IP54 rated for protection against dust and water 
ingress and meets multiple MIL-STD 810 tests for shock, vibration, and temperature resilience. It integrates with 
scalable MOTOTRBO systems including IP Site Connect, Capacity Plus, and optional trunking solutions, enabling 
expanded coverage and network flexibility across wide operational areas. The unit’s rugged construction, large channel 
capacity, and advanced digital features make the DM4600e a versatile communication solution for shipboard base 
stations, mobile dispatch, and industrial radio networks where dependable voice and data connectivity are critical. 
MOTOTRBO DM4600e installed at the following location: 
▪ 
NAV.DECK WHEELHOUSE (86-RX-484) 
▪ 
C-DECK OIM OFFICE (86-RX-485)

1.3.13 
UHF PORTABLE RADIO MOTOTRBO DP4801EX ATEX RADIO 
The Motorola MOTOTRBO DP4801EX is a rugged, intrinsically safe UHF portable two-way radio designed for use in 
hazardous environments where explosive gas, dust, or mining atmospheres are present. It is certified to ATEX and 
IECEx standards for gas and dust protection (Class II 2G Ex ib IIC T4 Gb; Class II 2D Ex ib IIIC T130 °C Db), making it 
suitable for offshore platforms, chemical processing areas, and other high-risk industrial zones.  
Operating in the UHF band (403–470 MHz), the DP4801EX supports up to 1000 programmable channels organized into 
zones, enabling flexible frequency planning and efficient communication across large teams. The unit features a full-
colour, multi-line display and an alphanumeric keypad for intuitive menu navigation, text messaging, and channel 
selection.  
The radio includes five programmable buttons, a prominent emergency button, and additional advanced features such 
as integrated GPS, transmit interrupt, lone worker and man-down alerts, and remote monitor capabilities to support 
operational safety and situational awareness. Its robust design provides IP67 ingress protection, ensuring dust and 
water resistance for use in harsh outdoor and marine conditions.  
Built-in digital enhancements such as Intelligent Audio, voice and data integration, and support for Motorola 
MOTOTRBO trunking and networking solutions (including direct mode, Capacity Plus, and IP Site Connect) make the 
DP4801EX a versatile and dependable communication tool for critical operations where reliable, clear, and secure 
radio communication is essential.

1.3.13 
UHF PORTABLE RADIO MOTOTRBO DP4801EX ATEX RADIO 
The Motorola MOTOTRBO DP4801EX is a rugged, intrinsically safe UHF portable two-way radio designed for use in 
hazardous environments where explosive gas, dust, or mining atmospheres are present. It is certified to ATEX and 
IECEx standards for gas and dust protection (Class II 2G Ex ib IIC T4 Gb; Class II 2D Ex ib IIIC T130 °C Db), making it 
suitable for offshore platforms, chemical processing areas, and other high-risk industrial zones.  
Operating in the UHF band (403–470 MHz), the DP4801EX supports up to 1000 programmable channels organized into 
zones, enabling flexible frequency planning and efficient communication across large teams. The unit features a full-
colour, multi-line display and an alphanumeric keypad for intuitive menu navigation, text messaging, and channel 
selection.  
The radio includes five programmable buttons, a prominent emergency button, and additional advanced features such 
as integrated GPS, transmit interrupt, lone worker and man-down alerts, and remote monitor capabilities to support 
operational safety and situational awareness. Its robust design provides IP67 ingress protection, ensuring dust and 
water resistance for use in harsh outdoor and marine conditions.  
Built-in digital enhancements such as Intelligent Audio, voice and data integration, and support for Motorola 
MOTOTRBO trunking and networking solutions (including direct mode, Capacity Plus, and IP Site Connect) make the 
DP4801EX a versatile and dependable communication tool for critical operations where reliable, clear, and secure 
radio communication is essential.

 MANUFACTURERS' MANUALS 
This section includes the manufacturers’ manuals for the installation, operation, and maintenance of the equipment. 
3.1 INSTALLATION INSTRUCTIONS 
3.1.1 
INSTALLATION INSTRUCTIONS OF UHF REPEATER MOTOTRBO SLR5500 
 
 
The Motorola MOTOTRBO SLR5500 is a high-performance, professional digital repeater designed to extend two-way 
UHF radio coverage and improve communication reliability in both analog and digital systems. It supports a wide range 
of network configurations including Digital Conventional, IP Site Connect, Capacity Plus, Linked Capacity Plus, and 
Connect Plus trunking architectures, enabling scalable and flexible system deployment. The repeater operates across 
the 403–470 MHz UHF band with an RF output power of up to 50 W and a 100 % duty cycle for continuous operation. 
It features a space-efficient 1U rack form factor with integrated power supply and charger, advanced digital error 
correction, clear voice quality, and robust diagnostics with status indicators. Support for mixed analog/digital modes 
allows seamless migration from older systems, while Ethernet connectivity and modular field-replaceable components 
enable remote management, system integration, and simplified maintenance.

• 
RF Combiner Connection  
The RF combiner integrates the transmit and receive signals from four UHF repeaters into a common antenna system 
to enable shared antenna operation while maintaining isolation between channels. 
▪ 
Each repeater TX output (TX1–TX4) connects to the corresponding TX input ports of the RF combiner. 
▪ 
Each repeater RX input (RX1–RX4) connects to the corresponding RX output ports of the combiner. 
▪ 
The combiner internally filters and isolates each channel to prevent intermodulation and mutual interference 
between repeaters. 
▪ 
The combined RF output (ANT port) is routed to the common UHF antenna feeder cable. 
▪ 
TX coupling and RX coupling test ports (TX COUP / RX COUP) are provided for monitoring forward and reflected 
power measurements during testing and maintenance. 
▪ 
The combiner operates from a 12 VDC power supply, sourced from the system PSU. 
▪ 
Patch cords (RG142/RG141) are used between repeaters and combiner to ensure low loss and proper 
impedance matching. 
▪ 
All RF connections use properly terminated coaxial cables and must be securely tightened to maintain signal 
integrity and minimize VSWR. 
The arrangement allows multiple repeaters to operate simultaneously ensuring stable, efficient, and interference-free 
system performance.

The Motorola DM4400e is a digital mobile radio installed as a fixed control station inside the UHF cabinet as part of 
the  
ship’s onboard UHF communication system. It is connected to the TRBO Net Server, which manages and routes all 
MOTOTRBO digital voice communications, enabling reliable two-way communication between the bridge/control 
room and portable or mobile radios used by ship personnel. Each radio is assigned a unique ID to allow clear, organized 
individual or group calls. 
Additional DM4400e units are interfaced with the ship’s PAGA Systems A and B to provide voice interconnection 
between the UHF network and the public address system for announcements and emergency broadcasts. Separate 
interfaces ensure redundancy and continuous operation. The fixed cabinet installation provides secure mounting, 
stable power, and dependable system performance.

3.1.4 
INSTALLATION INSTRUCTIONS OF KVM SWITCH NORCOSEA KVM-1701COMBO 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
The unit is a rack-mounted KVM console designed to provide centralized control and monitoring of connected 
computers from a single workstation. It features a 17-inch A-grade LED-backlit LCD display with a maximum resolution 
of 1280 × 1024, delivering clear and sharp visuals. The LCD panel can rotate up to 120 degrees to allow comfortable 
viewing and flexible positioning during operation. 
The console supports multiple computers through both PS/2 and USB interfaces and includes a front USB port for 
connecting an external keyboard or mouse when required. It is compatible with a wide range of operating systems, 
including DOS, Windows, NetWare, Unix, Linux, and Mac OS, ensuring broad system integration. No additional 
software is required, and computer selection can be easily performed using front push buttons, hotkeys, or the on-
screen display (OSD). 
Housed in a durable 1U, 19-inch metal rack-mount enclosure, the unit integrates a full-size keyboard and touchpad for 
convenient operation. The sliding rail design enables smooth pull-out access with a self-locking mechanism for stability. 
The keyboard layout can also be customized to meet specific language requirements, making the console suitable for 
various operational environments.

The onboard UHF communication network is centralized around a 24-port Ethernet network switch, which serves 
as the main connectivity hub for all IP-based equipment installed within the UHF cabinet. The switch facilitates high-
speed data communication between the TRBO Net Server PC, KVM switch with monitor, and all four UHF repeater 
interfaces. The TRBO Server PC connects to the network switch via an RJ45 Ethernet port, providing centralized 
control, routing, and monitoring of the MOTOTRBO digital radio network. The KVM switch, which includes an 
integrated monitor, connects to the switch to provide local management and configuration access for the server 
and connected devices. The TRBO server is linked to the KVM switch using an HDB-15 VGA connection along with 
USB ports, allowing full keyboard, video, and mouse control of the server through the KVM interface. 
All repeaters are connected to the network switch using CAT6 patch cords, ensuring reliable communication and stable 
system performance across the entire UHF network. This setup enables operators to manage the UHF radios, monitor 
system status, and perform configuration tasks centrally while maintaining organized cable routing, simplified 
troubleshooting, and dependable network operation for the vessel’s communication system. 
3.1.7 
INSTALLATION INSTRUCTIONS OF KBT GFQ-2-0305/ KBT GFQ-3-0305/ KBT OHQ-10-0305

GFQ-2-0305/ GFQ-3-0305

The KBT GFQ-0305 Series power splitters are passive RF components designed for dividing a single UHF signal into 
multiple output paths within the 300–500 MHz frequency range, making them suitable for indoor communication 
systems such as UHF antenna distribution and signal routing. The 2-way splitter (GFQ-2-0305) provides two equal 
output ports from one input, allowing one transmitter feed to be divided into two antenna or distribution lines with 
minimal insertion loss and maintained impedance matching. The 3-way splitter (GFQ-3-0305) similarly divides the input 
signal into three equal outputs, enabling one feed point to serve three antenna or branch connections while preserving 
signal integrity. Both models feature 50 Ω nominal impedance, N-female connectors, low insertion loss, and good 
isolation between ports to reduce mutual interference, supporting up to approximately 50 W in UHF applications.  
       In this shipboard UHF network, these splitters are used as signal power dividers to distribute 
transmitter output to multiple antenna paths for coverage and redundancy—specifically a total of 13 units of 
GFQ-2-0305 and 7 units of GFQ-3-0305 are deployed to achieve the required antenna network topology and ensure 
even power distribution across all connected antennas.

•
Mechanical Drawing
The KBT OHQ-10-0305 directional coupler is a passive UHF RF component used for controlled signal tapping and power 
distribution within the antenna network. Operating within the UHF frequency band and designed for 50 Ω systems, 
the coupler allows a defined portion of the RF power to be coupled from the main transmission line while maintaining 
low insertion loss and high isolation between ports. This ensures efficient signal distribution without significantly 
affecting the primary signal path. 
Within the onboard UHF communication system, two OHQ-10-0305 directional couplers are installed at different 
locations to support the antenna distribution architecture. The first unit is mounted inside the UHF equipment cabinet, 
where it is connected at the output of the RF combiner. Its function is to separate and route the main antenna output 
into the internal UHF antenna network, which consists of multiple 2-way and 3-way splitters. This arrangement enables 
controlled division of transmitter power to several antenna branches while maintaining impedance matching and 
minimizing signal reflections. 
The second coupler is installed in the E-House and is used to divide RF power between the locally installed E-House 
antenna and the main feeder line supplying the Bosun Store UHF antenna. This configuration ensures balanced signal 
distribution coverage for both areas without the need for additional active amplification. 
Together, these directional couplers provide reliable passive signal routing, improve network flexibility, and support 
stable and efficient operation of the ship’s UHF antenna system.

3.1.8 
INTERNAL CABINET LAYOUT AND EQUIPMENT MOUNTING 
The UHF communication system shall be installed within a floor-mounted 19-inch Rittal equipment cabinet located in 
A deck Telecom room. The Rittal cabinet (800×1000×2100) is a floor-standing industrial enclosure designed for safe 
installation and protection of electrical, networking, and communication equipment. Manufactured from powder-
coated steel, the cabinet offers high mechanical strength and corrosion resistance, making it suitable for marine and 
industrial environments. an indoor, controlled environment. The cabinet is mounted on a 100 mm base plinth and 
arranged to ensure proper ventilation, accessibility, cable management, and future expandability.  
All equipment is securely mounted using standard rack hardware. Power and signal cables are neatly dressed along 
the cabinet sides using vertical cable management channels. Adequate spacing and blank panels are maintained to 
support heat dissipation and simplify maintenance activities. 
The installation ensures a clean, organized, and serviceable cabinet layout compliant with best practices for indoor 
UHF communication system deployments. 
The cabinet layout is organized vertically, with equipment installed from top to bottom as follows:

3.1.8 
INTERNAL CABINET LAYOUT AND EQUIPMENT MOUNTING 
The UHF communication system shall be installed within a floor-mounted 19-inch Rittal equipment cabinet located in 
A deck Telecom room. The Rittal cabinet (800×1000×2100) is a floor-standing industrial enclosure designed for safe 
installation and protection of electrical, networking, and communication equipment. Manufactured from powder-
coated steel, the cabinet offers high mechanical strength and corrosion resistance, making it suitable for marine and 
industrial environments. an indoor, controlled environment. The cabinet is mounted on a 100 mm base plinth and 
arranged to ensure proper ventilation, accessibility, cable management, and future expandability.  
All equipment is securely mounted using standard rack hardware. Power and signal cables are neatly dressed along 
the cabinet sides using vertical cable management channels. Adequate spacing and blank panels are maintained to 
support heat dissipation and simplify maintenance activities. 
The installation ensures a clean, organized, and serviceable cabinet layout compliant with best practices for indoor 
UHF communication system deployments. 
The cabinet layout is organized vertically, with equipment installed from top to bottom as follows:

▪ 
UHF Repeaters (Mototrbo SLR5500) are mounted centrally within the rack to ensure balanced weight 
distribution and optimal RF cable routing. 
▪ 
An RF Combiner (UIY4T4R) is installed to combine multiple UHF repeater outputs into a common antenna 
feed, minimizing antenna infrastructure and insertion losses. 
▪ 
Cable management panels are provided above and below active equipment to ensure structured routing of 
RF, power, and data cables, preventing strain and signal interference. 
▪ 
A 24-port network switch (DGS-1024C) is installed to support IP connectivity for system monitoring, control, 
and integration with other communication systems. 
▪ 
A KVM switch (KVM-1701COMBO) is included to allow shared access to the server and control equipment for 
maintenance and configuration. 
▪ 
A TRBOnet server PC (HP ProDesk 400 G9 SFF i5) is installed to manage UHF radio network operations, logging, 
dispatching, and system diagnostics. 
▪ 
PAGA Interface A and B units are installed to provide integration between the UHF system and the Public 
Address & General Alarm (PAGA) system. 
▪ 
A Control Station Motorola  DM4400e is installed to allow direct operational control and monitoring of the 
UHF system. 
▪ 
MCB panels are mounted at the lower section of the cabinet to provide protected power distribution to all 
installed devices.

• 
Installation Sketch 
 
 
 
 
The Kenbotong TDJ-450BKB-Y is a 6 dBi outdoor UHF panel antenna designed for use in the 400–490 MHz band to 
support the vessel’s UHF communication network, providing enhanced directional coverage between onboard 
repeaters and the RF combiner system. 
For correct installation and optimum performance: 
▪ 
Select Mounting Location: Choose a clear installation location above deck or on a mast where the antenna can 
be oriented toward the intended coverage area. Ensure there are no nearby obstructions that could block or 
reflect RF signals. 
▪ 
Pole/Mast Mount: The antenna is supplied with a mount that fits a 40–50 mm diameter mast or pole. Securely 
attach the antenna bracket and tighten all fasteners to ensure mechanical stability in marine conditions. 
▪ 
Orientation: The antenna must be mounted vertically with the radiating surface facing the coverage area to 
maintain the intended vertical polarization and beam pattern. 
▪ 
Cable Routing: Connect the RF feeder cable (typically low-loss coax) to the N-female connector on the antenna. 
Route the cable down the mast, using proper weatherproofing and cable clamps to prevent strain and water 
ingress along the run. 
▪ 
Grounding/Protection: Ensure the antenna and its metal mount are DC grounded in accordance with vessel 
grounding practices to reduce noise and static, and to provide some protection against atmospheric 
discharges. Good grounding also helps maintain stable RF performance. 
▪ 
Tightening and Sealing: All mechanical and cable connections must be firmly tightened and sealed to withstand 
vibration, wind, and saltwater exposure. 
Once installed and connected to the UHF system via the RF combiner and repeater links, the TDJ-450BKB-Y ensures reliable 
directional RF coverage for the onboard UHF communication network, improving link quality and system reliability in outdoor 
operating conditions.

MOUNTING METHOD  
 
 
The Amphenol Procom CXL70-3LW/L is a rugged, omnidirectional UHF base station antenna designed for marine and outdoor 
communication systems. For optimum performance of the onboard UHF network, installation must ensure proper mechanical 
support, correct polarization, good grounding, and secure RF connections. 
 
 Select Mounting Location 
▪ 
Choose a high, clear position on the vessel to maximize line-of-sight coverage. 
▪ 
Avoid nearby obstructions (cranes, deck fittings, superstructure) that could create RF shadow zones or 
reflections.

401e437d-69a9-4f78-a069-636dd5a894f1.text:
EMEM FPSO 
WCPOKWTP-450-TE-(VAE) MN-021 
 
 
[Comments] 
Telecom System IOM – UHF SYSTEM 
Rev: B 
Date: 04.02.2026 
Page 35 of 56 
Status: IFR 
 
      
This document is the property of Contractor and should not be distributed, copied, quoted or reproduced in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, without the prior 
written consent of Contractor. This document is issued as an “uncontrolled copy” unless over-stamped “CONTROLLED COPY”. 
 Mounting the Antenna 
▪ 
The CXL70-3LW/L antenna is supplied with a lightweight LW mast mount made of epoxy-coated, non-
corrosive aluminium. 
▪ 
Attach the antenna bracket securely to a vertical or horizontal mast/pole within the recommended 
diameter range (typically 16–54 mm). 
▪ 
Use the supplied stainless-steel U-bolts and fasteners to clamp the mount firmly to the mast. 
▪ 
Ensure that the mast is properly supported and able to withstand vibration, wind, and sea motion without 
loosening. 
Orientation and Polarization 
▪ 
The antenna must be installed in a vertical orientation, maintaining correct vertical polarization for the 
UHF network. 
▪ 
Ensure the antenna is firmly vertical to keep the omnidirectional pattern consistent around the vessel. 
Cable Routing 
▪ 
Connect the RF feeder cable to the antenna’s N-type connector. 
▪ 
Route the coaxial cable down the mast using cable clamps or ties at regular intervals to prevent stress and 
chafing. 
▪ 
Where possible, run the cable internally through the mast or use suitable protective conduit. 
▪ 
Keep cable bends gentle to prevent excessive loss and damage. 
Grounding and Lightning Protection 
▪ 
All metal parts of the antenna and mount should be grounded to the vessel’s structural ground to help 
reduce noise from atmospheric discharges and static buildup. 
▪ 
Confirm that grounding connections are robust, corrosion-resistant, and compatible with marine 
grounding practices. 
Weatherproofing 
▪ 
Ensure all external connectors and cable joints are weatherproofed with proper sealing tape, boots, or 
IP66-rated enclosures to prevent water ingress. 
▪ 
Inspect and tighten all fasteners after initial installation and periodically during routine maintenance. 
Post Installation Checks 
▪ 
Verify the antenna is firmly secured and properly aligned. 
▪ 
Confirm continuity and VSWR at the feed point with an RF analyser. 
▪ 
Inspect the grounding connection and cable runs for mechanical security and water resistance.

Overall UHF antenna distribution  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
The onboard UHF communication system is arranged using a distributed antenna network architecture to provide 
reliable radio coverage throughout all decks and operational areas of the vessel. The system originates from the UHF 
system cabinet, which houses the repeaters, control station, network switch, server, combiners/splitters, and 
associated RF distribution equipment. 
RF signals from the UHF repeaters are routed through directional couplers, splitters, and transmission lines, then 
distributed via low-loss coaxial feeder cables through dedicated cable ducts to multiple antenna locations installed 
across the A-deck, B-deck, C-deck, main deck, raised deck, engine room, control room, transformer room, compressor 
areas, and other critical compartments. 
Antennas are strategically positioned to ensure: 
• 
Uniform signal coverage across accommodation, machinery, and operational spaces

•

Reliable communication in both indoor and enclosed areas 
Each antenna branch is clearly labelled and connected through structured cable routing to maintain organized 
installation, simplified maintenance, and efficient troubleshooting. The centralized cabinet-based distribution design 
enables easy control, monitoring, and expansion of the UHF network while ensuring stable RF performance across the 
entire vessel. 
 
3.1.11 
INSTALLATION INSTRUCTIONS OF TKASEA RF 1/2” 50Ω LEAKY CABLE SHF1 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
In addition to fixed antenna installations, distributed RF coverage for enclosed and compartmentalized areas is 
achieved using a TKASEA RF 1/2” 50 Ω leaky feeder cable (Model SHF1). The leaky feeder system is deployed to provide 
continuous and uniform UHF signal propagation along passageways, internal decks, and shielded spaces where 
conventional antennas are limited by structural attenuation.

Each antenna branch is clearly labelled and connected through structured cable routing to maintain organized 
installation, simplified maintenance, and efficient troubleshooting. The centralized cabinet-based distribution design 
enables easy control, monitoring, and expansion of the UHF network while ensuring stable RF performance across the 
entire vessel. 
 
3.1.11 
INSTALLATION INSTRUCTIONS OF TKASEA RF 1/2” 50Ω LEAKY CABLE SHF1 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
In addition to fixed antenna installations, distributed RF coverage for enclosed and compartmentalized areas is 
achieved using a TKASEA RF 1/2” 50 Ω leaky feeder cable (Model SHF1). The leaky feeder system is deployed to provide 
continuous and uniform UHF signal propagation along passageways, internal decks, and shielded spaces where 
conventional antennas are limited by structural attenuation.

The SHF1 leaky cable is installed along the A-Deck, B-Deck, C-Deck, Main Deck, 2nd Deck, and 3rd Deck cable routes, 
supported within existing cable trays and ducts. The cable functions as a distributed antenna, radiating RF energy 
continuously along its length to eliminate coverage gaps and ensure reliable portable radio communication throughout 
the vessel’s interior compartments. 
RF signals from the UHF repeaters are fed into the leaky cable network through properly rated splitters and directional 
couplers to maintain impedance matching and balanced power distribution. All terminations are completed with 50 Ω 
loads to minimize reflections and standing wave ratio (VSWR). Connector joints are sealed and mechanically secured 
to maintain environmental and electrical integrity. 
This distributed coverage method ensures stable signal levels, improved penetration through steel structures, and 
consistent communication performance across all designated decks, thereby supporting safe and uninterrupted 
operations.

3.1.12 
INSTALLATION INSTRUCTIONS OF UHF BASE STATION RADIO MOTOTRBO DM4600E 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
In addition to the main distributed antenna and leaky feeder coverage network, five fixed UHF radio stations are 
installed onboard to provide dedicated local voice communication at key operational locations throughout the vessel. 
Each station is equipped with a Motorola MOTOTRBO DM4600e digital mobile radio configured for fixed operation. 
The fixed radios are strategically located in operational and control areas to ensure immediate access to the UHF 
network for personnel responsible for navigation, machinery, and emergency coordination. Each unit is powered from 
the vessel’s mains supply through an individual power socket and dedicated power supply unit (PSU), ensuring stable 
and continuous operation. 
RF connectivity from each DM4600e is provided via 50 Ω RF400 coaxial cable routed to the respective antenna 
connection point. A lightning arrestor is installed in-line on each RF feeder to provide surge protection and safeguard 
the radio equipment from transient overvoltage or lightning-induced surges. All RF connections are terminated with 
appropriate connectors and grounded in accordance with marine installation practices to maintain impedance 
matching and minimize signal loss. 
The DM4600e radios operate within the MOTOTRBO digital UHF network and support both individual and group voice 
calls, enabling reliable communication with portable radios, repeaters, and the central control station. Front-panel 
controls, microphone, and speaker provide direct user operation for call initiation, channel selection, and monitoring. 
This fixed station arrangement ensures dependable, point-to-point and group communication capability independent 
of portable units, thereby supporting routine operations, watchkeeping, and emergency response functions. The 
installation provides localized communication access while maintaining integration with the overall onboard UHF 
system for seamless vessel-wide coverage.

3.2 OPERATION – UHF COMMUNICATION SYSTEM 
3.2.1 
OPERATION MANUAL FOR BASE STATION RADIO MOTOTRBO DM4600E 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Key 
Description 
POWER BUTTON 
Turns the radio on and off. 
VOLUME/CHANNEL KNOB 
Rotate clockwise to increase volume level; rotate counterclockwise to decrease 
volume level. Push knob to activate channel function; rotate clockwise and 
counterclockwise to select channel. 
LED INDICATORS 
Red, yellow and green light-emitting diodes indicate operating status. 
LCD 
160x72 display provides visual information about many radio features 
OK/MENU BUTTON 
One button provides menu navigation and selection interface. 
PROGRAMMABLE BUTTONS 
Four buttons are field programmable using the CPS. 
SCROLL UP/DOWN BUTTONS 
Press buttons to scroll. 
RETURN/HOME BUTTON 
One button which quickly brings you to the home page. 
 
▪ 
Push-To-Talk (PTT) Button 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
While a call is in progress, the PTT button allows the radio to transmit to other radios in the call. Press and hold down 
PTT button to talk. Release the PTT button to listen. The microphone is activated when the PTT button is pressed.

• 
LED Indicator 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Key 
Description 
Blinking Red 
Radio is transmitting at low battery condition, receiving an emergency transmission, 
has failed the self-test upon powering up, or has moved out of range if radio is 
configured with Auto-Range Transponder System. 
Solid Yellow 
Radio is monitoring a conventional channel or in Bluetooth Discoverable Mode. 
Blinking Yellow 
Radio is scanning for activity or receiving a Call Alert, flexible receive list is 
enabled or all local Linked Capacity Plus channels are busy. 
Double blinking Yellow 
Radio is no longer connected to the repeater while in Capacity Plus or Linked 
Capacity Plus; all Capacity Plus or Linked Capacity Plus channels are currently busy. 
Solid Green 
Radio is powering up or transmitting. 
Blinking Green 
Radio is powering up, receiving a non-privacy enabled call or data, or detecting 
activity, or retrieving Over-the-Air Programming transmissions over the air. 
Double blinking Green 
Radio is receiving a privacy-enabled call or data. 
 
• 
Powering Up the Radio 
 
 
Press the on/Off Button (A) briefly. You see MOTOTRBO (TM) on the radio’s display momentarily, 
followed by a welcome message or welcome image. The green LED and the Home screen light up. 
 
 
 
 
 
• 
Adjusting the Volume 
 
 
 
To increase the volume, turn the Volume/Channel Knob (A) clockwise. To decrease the volume, 
turn this knob counterclockwise.

• 
Using the Volume Knob to change the Channel 
 
To change channels, push and hold the knob until channel selection screen is displayed, then turn the knob to the required channel. 
 
• 
Selecting a Zone 
 
A zone is a group of channels. Your radio supports up to 99 channels and 2 zones, with a maximum of 99 channels per zone. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Press (OK) to select. The display shows Selected momentarily and returns to the selected zone screen. 
 
• 
Selecting a Channel 
 
Transmissions are sent and received on a channel. Depending on your radio’s configuration, each channel may have been 
programmed differently to support different groups of users or supplied with different features.  
Select a channel by using:  
▪ 
Scroll Up or Scroll Down buttons.  
▪ 
Volume/Channel Knob.  
▪ 
The programmed Channel Up or Channel Making and Receiving Calls in Non-Connect Plus Mode Down 
buttons 
 
• 
Making a Group Call 
 
▪ 
Press the Volume/Channel Knob to enter the channel selection state. 
▪ 
Turn the knob to select the channel with the active group alias or ID. 
▪ 
Press the PTT button to make the call. The green LED lights up. 
▪ 
Wait for the Talk Permit Tone to finish (if enabled) and speak clearly into the microphone or Wait for 
the PTT Sidetone to finish and speak clearly into the microphone. 
▪ 
Release the PTT button to listen. When the target radio responds, the green LED blinks.

To execute voice operations (for example, Group Call and Private Call).

Microphone

specific accessories will connect and be activated. 
13 
Emergency button 
To turn on and off the Emergency Operations. 
14 
Antenna 
Provides the needed RF amplification when transmitting or receiving.

Telecom System IOM – UHF SYSTEM 
Rev: B

Page 50 of 56 
Status: IFR 
 
      
This document is the property of Contractor and should not be distributed, copied, quoted or reproduced in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, without the prior 
written consent of Contractor. This document is issued as an “uncontrolled copy” unless over-stamped “CONTROLLED COPY”. 
Basic Call Handling 
▪ 
Selecting a channel or talk group is done via the channel selector or menu to choose the 
correct communication path before transmitting. 
▪ 
To make a call, press and hold the PTT (Push-To-Talk) button and speak clearly; release to 
listen. 
▪ 
The radio supports group calls, private calls, and phone calls (if enabled by system).  
Contact List and Manual Dial 
▪ 
A contact list stores radio IDs and contact names for quick access. 
▪ 
Users can navigate the contacts menu to select a contact and initiate a call. 
▪ 
Manual dialling allows input of a radio number directly from the keypad for ad-hoc calls.  
Call Alerts 
▪ 
Call Alerts notify a specific radio or group that someone is attempting to communicate. 
▪ 
Alerts can be generated from the contacts list or an assigned One Touch Access button. 
▪ 
When a call alert is sent, the radio’s display shows the alert and LED indicates transmission.  
Call Indicator Settings 
▪ 
Users can turn call alert tones on/off for incoming calls via the menu under Radio Settings → 
Tones/Alerts → Call Ringers. 
▪ 
Different ringing tones can be assigned for Group, Private, or Selective calls.  
Call Log and Notifications 
▪ 
The radio maintains a call log of received, sent, and missed calls. 
▪ 
Users can view details, delete entries, and respond to missed calls from the log.  
Emergency Operation 
▪ 
The emergency feature allows sending an emergency alarm with or without voice. 
▪ 
During Emergency mode the radio transmits repeatedly until acknowledged or the user exits 
emergency mode.  
Text Messaging 
▪ 
The DP4801EX supports text messages up to 140 characters. 
▪ 
Users can compose messages via the menu, select a contact or ID, and send messages. 
▪ 
Received messages are stored and can be replied to, forwarded, or deleted. 
 
3.2.3 
OVERALL SYSTEM OPERATION  
 
The onboard UHF communication system operates as an integrated digital MOTOTRBO radio network designed to 
provide continuous, vessel-wide voice communication between portable radios, fixed radios, and control stations. The 
system comprises multiple UHF repeaters, an RF combining and distribution network, distributed antenna/leaky feeder 
coverage, fixed mobile radios, and centralized monitoring and control equipment installed within the UHF system 
cabinet. 
During normal operation, each UHF repeater receives RF signals transmitted by portable or fixed radios within its 
coverage area. The received signals are processed and retransmitted at the assigned output frequency to extend 
communication range and ensure reliable coverage throughout all decks and compartments. This repeater-based 
architecture eliminates dead zones and maintains consistent signal strength across the vessel. 
All repeaters transmit outputs are routed to the RF combiner unit installed inside the UHF cabinet. The combiner 
consolidates multiple repeater RF outputs into a single low-loss feed while maintaining proper impedance matching

539d4507-7c38-4568-9c8d-abcd243796d6.text:
EMEM FPSO 
WCPOKWTP-450-TE-(VAE) MN-021 
 
 
[Comments] 
Telecom System IOM – UHF SYSTEM 
Rev: B 
Date: 04.02.2026 
Page 51 of 56 
Status: IFR 
 
      
This document is the property of Contractor and should not be distributed, copied, quoted or reproduced in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, without the prior 
written consent of Contractor. This document is issued as an “uncontrolled copy” unless over-stamped “CONTROLLED COPY”. 
and isolation between channels. This prevents intermodulation and mutual interference between repeaters. The 
combined RF signal is then distributed through the antenna/leaky feeder network to provide uniform coverage along 
A-Deck, B-Deck, C-Deck, Main Deck, 2nd Deck, and 3rd Deck. 
Inbound RF signals from the antenna system follow the reverse path through the combiner/splitter network and are 
directed to the respective repeaters for processing. Directional couplers and splitters installed within the cabinet 
ensure correct signal distribution and controlled power levels across the network. 
Five fixed MOTOTRBO DM4600e radios installed at designated operational areas provide permanent communication 
points. These radios operate on the same UHF channels as the repeater network and allow users to communicate 
directly with portable radios and other fixed stations. Each fixed station is connected via 50-ohm RF400 coaxial cable 
and protected by inline lightning arrestors to safeguard equipment against surge and transient voltages. 
System management and configuration are performed through the TRBO Server PC located inside the cabinet. The 
server interfaces with the repeaters via the Ethernet network switch, enabling centralized monitoring, programming, 
diagnostics, and logging of repeater and network parameters. The integrated KVM switch and monitor provide local 
access to the server for maintenance, troubleshooting, and software configuration. Video connectivity is provided via 
HDB-15 VGA, while keyboard and mouse control are provided through USB/PS2 interfaces. 
All IP-based equipment, including the server and management interfaces, is interconnected using CAT6 patch cables 
through the 24-port network switch to ensure reliable and high-speed communication. This centralized architecture 
simplifies cable routing, improves system organization, and supports efficient fault diagnosis.

3.3 MAINTENANCE 
The onboard UHF communication system requires periodic preventive maintenance to ensure continuous, reliable, 
and interference-free operation. Maintenance activities shall be carried out by qualified personnel in accordance with 
marine electrical safety procedures. All inspections and test results shall be recorded in the vessel’s maintenance log. 
Before performing any maintenance work, isolate the relevant equipment from the power supply and ensure RF 
transmission is disabled where applicable. 
 
UHF Repeaters 
▪ 
Verify normal operation status indicators (Power, TX, RX, Alarm LEDs). 
▪ 
Check for abnormal noise, overheating, or warning alarms. 
▪ 
Confirm proper ventilation and ensure cooling airflow is unobstructed. 
RF Combiner and Distribution Network 
▪ 
Inspect RF connectors for tightness and corrosion. 
▪ 
Check that all 50 Ω terminations are secure. 
▪ 
Verify no physical damage to coaxial cables. 
Leaky Feeder and Antenna Network 
▪ 
Inspect accessible cable routes for mechanical damage or loose supports. 
▪ 
Ensure cable clamps and supports remain secure. 
▪ 
Check lightning arrestors for grounding integrity and corrosion. 
Fixed Radios (MOTOTRBO DM4600e) 
▪ 
Perform transmit and receive test on assigned channels. 
▪ 
Check microphone, speaker, and display operation. 
▪ 
Verify power supply stability and no loose connections. 
Server, Switch, and KVM 
▪ 
Confirm server is operational with no system alarms. 
▪ 
Verify repeater connectivity via network monitoring interface. 
▪ 
Inspect Ethernet connections and cable management. 
▪ 
Confirm KVM functionality for local access. 
RF Performance Verification 
▪ 
Measure VSWR at main RF output point. 
▪ 
Verify forward and reflected power levels are within acceptable limits. 
▪ 
Check signal strength at representative deck locations. 
Repeater Operation Test 
▪ 
Perform on-air test for each repeater channel. 
▪ 
Confirm clear audio quality without distortion or interference. 
▪ 
Verify group and individual call functionality. 
Fixed Radio Communication Test 
▪ 
Conduct communication test between all fixed radios. 
▪ 
Confirm integration with repeater network. 
▪ 
Verify emergency communication procedures.

▪ 
Verify power supply stability and no loose connections. 
Server, Switch, and KVM 
▪ 
Confirm server is operational with no system alarms. 
▪ 
Verify repeater connectivity via network monitoring interface. 
▪ 
Inspect Ethernet connections and cable management. 
▪ 
Confirm KVM functionality for local access. 
RF Performance Verification 
▪ 
Measure VSWR at main RF output point. 
▪ 
Verify forward and reflected power levels are within acceptable limits. 
▪ 
Check signal strength at representative deck locations. 
Repeater Operation Test 
▪ 
Perform on-air test for each repeater channel. 
▪ 
Confirm clear audio quality without distortion or interference. 
▪ 
Verify group and individual call functionality. 
Fixed Radio Communication Test 
▪ 
Conduct communication test between all fixed radios. 
▪ 
Confirm integration with repeater network. 
▪ 
Verify emergency communication procedures.
